Skip to main content
⚠️
Spot-Linked Reference Estimates: These prices are based on global XAU/USD spot data and conversion rules, not final retail jewelry quotes (which may add making charges, dealer/shop margins, and local taxes). Freshness labels follow one shared vocabulary: Live, Delayed, Cached/Fallback, Estimated, and Historical baseline. See Methodology for definitions and source details.
📈 Live tab — current prices for 24+ countries ⚖️ Compare tab — compare regions side-by-side 📋 Planner — budget & Zakat calculators
Preparing live feed Gold market Waiting for first sync

Gold Tracker Pro

First action sequence: refresh live reference prices, choose your market + karat, then read the chart before opening advanced tools.

Jump to chart

Start with one path

  1. Press Refresh live data and confirm the source badge shows Live or Cached/Fallback.
  2. Pick your market, currency, karat, and unit in the live controls.
  3. Read the chart trend and decision cues, then open advanced workspace only when needed.

All figures here are spot-linked reference estimates, not final retail jewelry quotes.

Keyboard: R refresh · H Live · C Compare · A Alerts · P Planner

Live desk summary

Workspace tips

  • Use the tabs below to switch between Live, Compare, Archive, Alerts, Planner, Exports, and Method.
  • Watchlists, alerts, and presets stay in your browser only — no account required.
  • Exports always include timestamps, range, and source-layer labels for trust and reuse.
  • Method explains exactly what is live, cached, estimated, or synthetic in each view.
Market wire
Live headlines · waiting…
Loading live market headlines…

Live chart workspace

Blended spot-linked history: recent ranges use live and cached snapshots; longer ranges use monthly baseline anchored to XAU/USD.

Selected series

Range playback

Visible range notes

Karat ladder & watchlist

Fast context for your selected view across major karats and favorite markets.

Karat Purity Selected price vs 24K

Market watchlist

Decision cues

Markets compare board

Live-ranking board for GCC, Arab, and reference markets with favorites, filters, and deep links to country pages.

No markets match the current filters.

Alerts + presets

Browser-based price alerts and view presets — stored locally. The live chart and prices remain visible behind this panel.

New alert

Saved alerts

Save preset

Saved presets

Planners + estimators

Turn live prices into decisions. The chart and prices remain visible behind this panel.

Budget planner

Position tracker

Jewelry ticket estimate

Accumulation planner

Open full calculator →

Archive + lookup

Browse unified history (baseline + cache), export slices, and jump to the nearest archived point for any date.

Archive browser

Date Spot USD/oz Selected view UAE 24K/g Source

Date lookup

Find the closest data point for any date in the archive. Searches baseline + session data.

Seasonal patterns

Exports

Trustworthy CSV and JSON exports with clear source and timestamp metadata for the current workspace.

Visible chart CSV

Exports only the currently visible chart slice with range, mode, and source-layer annotations.

Current view CSV

Snapshot of the current currency/karat/unit across markets, matching the compare board ordering.

Archive CSVs

Download cached daily archive and full baseline+cache history for longer-term analysis.

Snapshot JSON

Structured JSON payload with prices, FX, favorites, and selected view — useful for tooling integration.

Market brief text

Export the current market brief as plain text, suitable for email or note-taking.

Methodology + sources

This workspace is explicit about what is live, what is cached, what is estimated, and what is synthetic baseline.

Live spot layer

Live spot is pulled from Gold API in XAU/USD and refreshed roughly every 90 seconds, with retries and fallbacks from cache when networks fail.

Gold API docs

FX conversion layer

FX conversion comes from ExchangeRate-API’s USD base feed. AED uses the fixed central-bank peg stored in the site constants, not the API feed.

ExchangeRate-API docs

Historical layer

Short ranges draw on recent live snapshots cached in your browser. Longer ranges use a monthly baseline merged with those snapshots to avoid gaps and keep exports stable.

DataHub gold dataset

Retail vs bullion

All prices are bullion-equivalent estimates based on spot. Actual jewelry and retail prices can differ due to making charges, premiums, and taxes.

Read full site methodology

News wire

The market wire uses the GDELT DOC API in article-list mode, suitable for static sites. When it fails, the workspace falls back to cached headlines or a static explainer strip.

GDELT DOC API

Market brief

One-paragraph summary of current gold conditions for your selected view, updated as you change markets, karats, and time ranges.
